title: Multi-target delivery
principles: Multi-target delivery describes explicit mechanisms that allow one attack mode to apply valid damage to more than one target.
Radial coverage, chained propagation, linear penetration, and distributed instances are distinct mechanisms and must remain separately traceable.
Target count, spatial reach, geometric restrictions, and retained damage are separate properties and must not be collapsed into one multi-target score.
A fixed target limit may be derived only when the mechanic explicitly supplies one; radial coverage and Punch Through do not inherently provide a fixed enemy count.
Instance multiplicity supports multi-target delivery only when independently validated distribution, trajectory, spread, or targeting evidence allows different targets to be reached.
Multi-target capability does not by itself establish group-clear reliability, area control, or equal value across reached targets.
Each mechanism must remain associated with the attack mode and damage component that supplies its evidence.

radial_coverage: An explicit radial component can apply damage within a supplied spatial region around its origin.
Radius, falloff, line-of-sight behavior, and other spatial restrictions must remain separately represented when supplied.
chained_propagation: The attack explicitly propagates from an initial target to one or more additional targets.
Additional target count, chain range, range origin, and damage retention must remain separate.
linear_penetration: An eligible attack component can continue through targets or surfaces along its path.
Multi-target reach remains conditional on alignment, collision eligibility, and available Punch Through depth rather than a fixed target count.
distributed_instances: Several independently validated instances can be distributed across different targets through supplied spread, trajectory, targeting, or allocation behavior.
combined_multi_target: The evaluated mode contains more than one supported multi-target mechanism.
Do not merge their target limits, spatial restrictions, origins, or damage-retention rules.
explicit_single_target: The supplied mechanic explicitly restricts the evaluated component to one valid target.
This classification requires positive limiting evidence and must not be inferred from absent multi-target fields.
undetermined: The supplied evidence does not establish whether or how the evaluated mode can apply valid damage to more than one target.

radial_component_present: Do not assume uniform damage, unlimited enemy count, unobstructed coverage, or explosion behavior.
Associate radius and falloff only with the radial component that supplies them.
chaining_present: Interpret chain target count as additional targets only when the source explicitly excludes the initial target.
Do not assume equal damage across targets or constant retention across every propagation step.
punch_through_present: Treat Punch Through as penetration depth rather than a guaranteed number of targets.
Do not assume that a related radial component follows the penetrating projectile or activates at every penetrated target.
distributed_instance_profile_present: Use distributed instances as multi-target evidence only when independent contact and cross-target distribution are supported.
Do not infer distribution from pellet count, multishot, or spread terminology alone.
multiple_mechanisms_present: Preserve a separate record for each mechanism and affected component.
Do not use one mechanism's target count, range, or retention rules to fill another mechanism's missing values.

exceptions: Do not infer multi-target delivery from a generic aoe, explosive, or special-mechanic label without explicit mechanical evidence.
Do not infer group-clear or area-control function from multi-target capability alone.
Do not assume that all reached targets share identical damage, critical, status, falloff, or eligibility behavior.
Do not convert Punch Through depth, radial radius, or instance count into a universal target count.
Do not merge multi-target evidence across separate attack modes or damage components.
